title: Syncope

items.final_wording: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1–1.1.2.1 — initial history, witness account, examination, and vital signs

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

items.rationale: Supports mapped TLoC terms, event and witness history, assessed negatives, neurological finding, examination, presets, and history.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-performed or reviewed 12-lead ECG and selected additional tests such as glucose or haemoglobin when indicated by the assessment.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-performed or reviewed 12-lead ECG and selected additional tests such as glucose or haemoglobin when indicated by the assessment.

items.rationale: Supports mapped ECG, glucose, and selected blood tests.

items.final_wording: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4.1–1.1.4.6 — red flags and urgent cardiovascular assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

items.rationale: Supports mapped exertional TLoC, family sudden death, urgent referral, and cardiology follow-up.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

items.final_wording: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.
